title: Gareth Thomas (rugby, born 1974)

teamA = [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] | appearancesA = 4 | pointsA = 12 }} }} '''Gareth Thomas''' {{postnominals|country=GBR|size=100%|CBE}} (born 25 July 1974){{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/wales/15442570#:~:text=%22We%20can%20confirm%20that%20Gareth,career%20for%20a%20remarkable%20person. |title=Gareth Thomas retires from rugby |publisher=[[BBC Sport]] |date=25 October 2011 |access-date=18 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180103213317/https://www.bbc.com/sport/wales/15442570 |archive-date=3 January 2018}} is a Welsh former professional [[rugby union]] and [[rugby league]] player, who represented Wales in both codes. Nicknamed "Alfie", he was the first Wales rugby union player to play in 100 test matches, and is currently the sixth-most capped Wales player. He is 15th among [[list of leading Rugby union Test try scorers|international try scorers]], and is the third-highest Wales try scorer. He also won four rugby league caps for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], scoring three tries. He played rugby union as a fullback, [[wing (rugby union)|wing]] or [[centre (rugby union)|centre]] for [[Bridgend RFC|Bridgend]], [[Cardiff RFC|Cardiff]], the [[Celtic Warriors]], [[Stade Toulousain|Toulouse]], [[Cardiff Blues]], and [[Wales national rugby union team|Wales]]. In 2010 he changed codes to rugby league when he transferred from Cardiff Blues to the [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] in [[Super League]], and later played for Wales. He retired from rugby in October 2011.{{cite news |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyunion/international/wales/8848154/Wales-international-Gareth-Thomas-announces-retirement-from-all-forms-of-rugby.html |title=Wales international Gareth Thomas announces retirement from all forms of rugby |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London |date=25 October 2011 |access-date=18 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111025165917/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyunion/international/wales/8848154/Wales-international-Gareth-Thomas-announces-retirement-from-all-forms-of-rugby.html |archive-date=25 October 2011}} Thomas [[coming out|came out]] as [[homosexuality|gay]] in December 2009.{{cite magazine |url=http://sportsillustrated.cnn.com/vault/article/magazine/MAG1168953/index.htm |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100501013846/http://sportsillustrated.cnn.com/vault/article/magazine/MAG1168953/index.htm |url-status=dead |archive-date=1 May 2010 |title=Gareth Thomas… The Only Openly Gay Male Athlete |first=Gary |last=Smith |author-link=Gary Smith (sportswriter) |magazine=[[Sports Illustrated]] |date=3 May 2010 |access-date=12 June 2010 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/dec/19/gareth-thomas-gay |title=Wales rugby legend Gareth Thomas comes out as gay |date=18 December 2009 |website=The Guardian |access-date=18 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150819073213/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/dec/19/gareth-thomas-gay |archive-date=19 August 2015}} The following year he was voted the most influential gay person in the UK in ''[[The Independent on Sunday]]'' [[Pink List]]{{cite news |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/people/news/the-iiosi-pink-list-2010-2040472.html |title=The ''IoS'' Pink List 2010 |first=Montgomery |last=Hugh |work=[[The Independent]] |date=1 August 2010 |access-date=11 November 2010 }} and received [[Stonewall (charity)|Stonewall]]'s Hero of the Year award.{{cite web |url=https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/rugby-star-gareth-stonewalls-hero-1884126 |title=Rugby star Gareth is Stonewall's hero of the year |last=Law |first=Peter |date=6 November 2010 |website=Wales Online |access-date=18 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201109003357/https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/rugby-star-gareth-stonewalls-hero-1884126 |archive-date=9 November 2020}} ==Rugby union== ===Club career=== Thomas started his career at [[Pencoed RFC]] at youth level before starting his first class career at [[Bridgend RFC|Bridgend]]. He then transferred to [[Pontypridd RFC|Pontypridd]], but never actually played a game for them before re-signing for Bridgend. He then spent a spell at [[Cardiff RFC|Cardiff]] starting in 1997 before rejoining hometown club Bridgend again in 2001 and captaining them to a [[Welsh Premier Division]] title in 2003, in a campaign where they were unbeaten at home and only lost to runners-up [[Neath RFC|Neath]] and [[Cardiff RFC|Cardiff]] away.{{cite news| url = https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/my_club/cardiff_blues/6281855.stm| publisher=BBC News| title=Blues seek early Thomas release| date=21 January 2007}} He then joined the [[Celtic Warriors]] once the [[Welsh Rugby Union]] implemented its regional rugby plans for the 2003–04 season. After one season as captain of the Warriors, the region was disbanded by the then-WRU chief [[David Moffett]], and owner Leighton Samuel, although Thomas had already agreed a deal to join French club [[Stade Toulousain|Toulouse]] where he was seen as the ideal replacement for soon-to-retire captain [[Émile Ntamack]]. He helped the club to a [[Heineken Cup]] victory in 2005 after a victory over [[Stade Français]] at [[Murrayfield Stadium|Murrayfield]]. On 20 January 2007, [[Cardiff Blues]] completed a deal to bring Thomas back to Wales for the 2007–08 season.{{cite web |url=https://www.nationalworld.com/news/people/gareth-thomas-wales-rugby-captain-hiv-abuse-3834658 |title=Gareth Thomas: who is ex Wales rugby captain, what has he said about HIV diagnosis, was he spat at in street? |last=Leylan |first=Kurtis |date=8 September 2022 |website=National World |access-date=18 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220908184537/https://www.nationalworld.com/news/people/gareth-thomas-wales-rugby-captain-hiv-abuse-3834658 |archive-date=8 September 2022}} ===International career=== Gareth Thomas made his début for Wales on 27 May 1995 against {{nrut|Japan}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20051230194347/http://uk.sports.yahoo.com/ru/si/profiles/thomas.html Profile] on ''[[Yahoo!]] Sport'' He scored a Welsh record-equalling four tries in the match against {{nrut|Italy}} in [[Treviso]] in 1999; one of only seven players to achieve that feat for Wales. He held the Wales record for the most international tries with 40 until [[Shane Williams]] surpassed that total in the [[2008 Six Nations Championship]] He surpassed the try record previously held by [[Ieuan Evans]] in 2004 against Italy. He also got a [[hat-trick]] of tries in the Second Test against [[Japan national rugby union team|Japan]] in 2001; 51 appearances after announcing his arrival on the international scene with a hat-trick in the [[1995 Rugby World Cup|1995 World Cup]] game against the same opposition. Equally at home at centre, it was from that position he scored the longest interception try ever seen at the [[Cardiff Arms Park|Arms Park]], a 90-metre dash against {{nrut|Australia}} in 1996. Thomas was selected for the [[2005 British & Irish Lions tour to New Zealand]].{{Cite web |title=Sportsfile – New Zealand v British and Irish Lions 2nd Test – 181877 |url=https://www.sportsfile.com/id/181877/ |access-date=2024-03-15 |website=www.sportsfile.com |language=en}} Following injury to [[Brian O'Driscoll]] in the opening minutes of the first test against {{nrut|New Zealand}}, he was made captain for the second and third tests of the series, becoming the ninth Welsh [[Captain (sports)|captain]] in Lions' history. In 2005 Thomas was found guilty of assault while playing rugby in France{{cite news| url = https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/uk_news/wales/4422684.stm| publisher=BBC News| title=Rugby captain guilty of assault| date=10 November 2005}} and in 2007 was banned for four weeks for misconduct charges.{{cite news| url = https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/my_club/ulster/6300155.stm| publisher=BBC News| title=Wales rule out Thomas ban appeal| date=2 February 2007}} Thomas was named as captain for Wales' final match in the [[2007 Six Nations Championship|2007 Six Nations]] against England after current captain [[Stephen Jones (rugby union)|Stephen Jones]] was ruled out with a broken wrist. When Thomas took the field, he equalled [[Gareth Llewellyn]]'s Wales record of 92 caps.{{cite web |url=http://wru.co.uk/2_9844.php |title=Thomas to deputise for injured captain |work=[[Welsh Rugby Union]] |date=14 March 2007 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070318231740/http://www.wru.co.uk/2_9844.php |archive-date=18 March 2007 }} He broke Llewellyn's record when he led Wales out against Australia at [[Telstra Stadium]] in Sydney on 26 May 2007, a match that Wales lost 29–23 on a Wallabies try after the siren.{{cite news |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/welsh/6691839.stm |title=Australia 29–23 Wales |date=26 May 2007 |access-date=26 May 2007 |publisher=[[BBC Sport]] }} His 100th and last test match was in his team's defeat by Fiji in the [[2007 Rugby World Cup|2007 World Cup]].{{cite web |url=https://punditarena.com/rugby/eoin-harte/gareth-thomas-newspaper-threatened-out-sexuality/ |title=Gareth Thomas reveals a newspaper threatened to out his sexuality in 2007 |last=Harte |first=Eoin |date=5 January 2022 |website=Pundit Arena |access-date=18 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220105122622/https://punditarena.com/rugby/eoin-harte/gareth-thomas-newspaper-threatened-out-sexuality/ |archive-date=5 January 2022}} ==Rugby league == Thomas joined [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] in March 2010 on an 18-month deal. He made his Crusaders' (and rugby league) début against French side [[Catalans Dragons]] on 19 March 2010. Thomas was concussed seconds into his rugby league début after a heavy challenge from Catalans player [[David Ferriol]], and as a result had to leave the field after thirty minutes. Crusaders went on to win the game 14–6. Thomas went on to score his first try for the Crusaders against [[Wakefield Trinity]] in a 20–10 away victory on 11 April.{{cite news |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/8607500.stm |title=Wakefield Trinity Wildcats 10–20 Crusaders |publisher=[[BBC News]] |date=11 April 2010 |access-date=9 August 2010 }} A groin injury prematurely ended Thomas' first season in rugby league, but his recovery was hoped to be complete in time for the [[2010 European Cup]], for which Thomas was selected in the [[Wales national rugby league team|Welsh]] preliminary squad.{{cite news |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8974832.stm |title=Gareth Thomas hails rugby hero Allan Bateman |publisher=[[BBC News]] |date=7 September 2010 |access-date=17 September 2010 }} In a warm-up test before the European Cup, he made a try-scoring international rugby league début in [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] 13–6 defeat by [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] in October 2010.{{cite news |url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/10/10/06/manual_213349.html |title=Thomas scores in Wales defeat |work=[[Sporting Life (British newspaper)|Sporting Life]] |date=6 October 2010 |archive-date=7 October 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111007134309/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague%2F10%2F10%2F06%2Fmanual_213349.html |url-status=dead }} He scored again in his next international vs Scotland.{{Cite news |last=Wilson |first=Andy |date=2010-10-10 |title=Gareth Thomas scores a try as Wales beat Scotland in European Cup |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/11/gareth-thomas-wales-scotland-european-cup |access-date=2024-03-15 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}} In the final game of the tournament, Thomas was appointed captain, filling in for the injured [[Lee Briers]]. He scored a try in that game, helping Wales to a one-point win for both the European Cup Trophy and a spot in the [[2011 Rugby League Four Nations]]. On 9 July 2011, Thomas confirmed on Twitter that he had broken his left arm during the 38–10 defeat by [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], and expected to be sidelined for two months.{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14095086 |title=Crusaders' Gareth Thomas could miss rest of season |publisher=[[BBC News]] |date=10 July 2011 |access-date=10 July 2011 }} After missing the rest of the season and failing to reach 100% fitness before the [[2011 Rugby League Four Nations]] tournament, on 25 October 2011 Thomas announced his immediate retirement.{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/25/gareth-thomas-retires-rugby-league-union |title=Wales international Gareth Thomas retires from all forms of rugby |work=[[The Guardian]] |date=25 October 2011 |access-date=25 October 2011 |first=Gregg |last=Roughley }} ==Personal life== Thomas was born in [[Sarn, Bridgend|Sarn]] near [[Bridgend]] in July 1974. In 2001 he married Jemma Thomas, whom he met when both were teenagers, in [[St Brides Major]], near Bridgend.{{cite web |url=http://sports.rightpundits.com/?p=5881 |title=Jemma Thomas: Gareth Thomas' Ex-Wife |work=Right Fielders |date=16 February 2011 |access-date=17 June 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130507054151/http://sports.rightpundits.com/?p=5881 |archive-date=7 May 2013 |url-status=dead }} During the marriage, Jemma suffered three [[miscarriage]]s.{{cite news |url=https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/third-baby-tragedy-alfie-jemma-2342069 |title=Third baby tragedy for Alfie and Jemma |work=[[Wales Online]] |date=3 April 2006 |access-date=25 December 2019 }} They filed for a divorce in 2007, which was finalised in 2009. In December 2009, Thomas publicly announced that he is [[homosexual|gay]].{{cite news |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/welsh/8421956.stm |publisher=[[BBC News]] |title=Ex-Lion Thomas reveals he is gay |date=19 December 2009 }}{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Dumont |title=Rugby Star Gareth Thomas Confirms He is Gay |url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/311203-rugby-star-gareth-thomas-confirms-he-is-gay |access-date=2023-09-06 |website=Bleacher Report |language=en}} He told the ''[[Daily Mail]]'', "I don't want to be known as a gay rugby player. I am a rugby player, first and foremost. I am a man". Thomas's public confirmation of his sexuality made him the first openly gay professional rugby union player.{{cite news |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyunion/international/britishandirishlionsrugby/6841818/Wales-and-Lions-full-back-Gareth-Thomas-reveals-he-is-gay.html |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London |first=Graham |last=Clutton |title=Wales and Lions full-back Gareth Thomas discloses he is homosexual |date=18 December 2009 }}{{cite news |url=https://www.pressgazette.co.uk/welsh-rugby-star-gareth-thomas-says-journalist-revealed-his-hiv-status-to-his-parents/ |title=Welsh rugby star Gareth Thomas says journalist revealed his HIV status to his parents |first=Freddy |last=Mayhew |work=[[Press Gazette]] |date=18 September 2019 |access-date=25 December 2019 }}{{Cite web |date=2010-01-30 |title=Gareth Thomas on the joy of coming out |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/home-news/gareth-thomas-on-the-joy-of-coming-out-1883659.html |access-date=2023-09-06 |website=The Independent |language=en}} In an interview with the BBC, Thomas talked about how he hoped that his coming out would mean that in the future, a young gay rugby player would be able to come out and be accepted as a "talented gay rugby player".{{cite news |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_union/welsh/8425335.stm |title=In-depth interview – Gareth Thomas |date=22 December 2009 |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=22 December 2009 }} Thomas also said, "What I choose to do when I close the door at home has nothing to do with what I have achieved in rugby".{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2009/dec/19/gay-groups-applaud-gareth-thomas |title=Gay activists praise rugby star Gareth Thomas's decision to come out |first=Jamie |last=Doward |date=19 December 2009 |access-date=16 June 2010 |work=[[The Guardian]] }} Since then, Thomas has been a vocal supporter of the [[NSPCC]] and [[ChildLine]], a telephone counselling service operated by the NSPCC for children and young people.{{cite news |url=https://rugbyspeakersuk.com/rugby-business-motivation/gareth-thomas |title=Gareth Thomas |work=Rugby Speakers |publisher=Champion Speakers |date=2018 |access-date=26 December 2019 }}{{cite web |url=https://resiconf.com/resiconvention2019/en/node/speaker-gareth-thomas |title=Gareth Thomas |publisher=RESI Convention 2019 |date=2019 |access-date=26 December 2019 }}{{cite news |url=https://www.homesandproperty.co.uk/luxury/property/former-wales-captain-gareth-thomas-is-renting-out-his-home-for-the-rugby-world-cup-48756.html |title=Former Wales captain Gareth Thomas is renting out his home for the Rugby World Cup |first=Meghann |last=Murdock |work=[[Evening Standard]]|location=London |date=8 September 2015 |access-date=26 December 2019 }} In an interview, Thomas commented, "I don't know if my life is going to be easier because I'm out, but if it helps someone else, if it makes one young lad pick up the phone to ChildLine, then it will have been worth it". He had a major health scare in February 2006. He had received a blow to his neck during a match; then later on while watching a televised recording of an interview he had given regarding his part in the resignation of Welsh coach [[Mike Ruddock]], he fell ill and was rushed to hospital with a suspected [[stroke]], which was brought on by a ruptured artery in his neck.{{cite web |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/wales/southeast/halloffame/sport/gareth_thomas.shtml |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090102045540/http://www.bbc.co.uk/wales/southeast/halloffame/sport/gareth_thomas.shtml |url-status=dead |archive-date=2 January 2009 |title=Gareth Thomas BBC profile |publisher=[[BBC Sport]] }} It was feared that this could lead to his retirement,{{cite news |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/international/4815772.stm |publisher=[[BBC News]] |title=Thomas rejects retirement reports |date=17 March 2006 }} but he resumed playing at the start of the 2006–07 French rugby season.{{Cite news |date=2006-11-01 |title=Stroke victim Thomas back for Wales |url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2006-11-01/stroke-victim-thomas-back-for-wales/1299632 |access-date=2024-03-15 |work=ABC News |language=en-AU}} His way of celebrating tries by slapping his head comes from a popular celebration among [[Cardiff City F.C.|Cardiff City]] supporters known as "[[The Ayatollah (football celebration)|the Ayatollah]]".{{cite news |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/international/3701742.stm |publisher=[[BBC News]] |title=Thomas turns gamekeeper |date=29 September 2004 |first=Sean |last=Davies }} His autobiography ''Proud'', co-written with Michael Calvin, won [[British Sports Book Awards|Sports Book of the Year]] in 2015.{{cite news |url=http://www.thesportsbookshelf.com/2015/06/gareth-thomas-autobiography-proud-is.html |work=The Sports Bookshelf |title=Gareth Thomas autobiography ''Proud'' is Sports Book of the Year after winning public vote |date=19 June 2015 |access-date=17 April 2018 |archive-date=18 April 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180418032949/http://www.thesportsbookshelf.com/2015/06/gareth-thomas-autobiography-proud-is.html |url-status=dead }}{{Cite web |title=Proud |url=https://sportsbookawards.com/winner/proud/ |access-date=2023-09-06 |website=Sports Book Awards |language=en-GB}} On 17 November 2018, Thomas was assaulted in Cardiff [[gay bashing|because of his sexuality]]. He requested that [[South Wales Police]] deal with the 16-year-old assailant by way of [[restorative justice]].{{cite news |title=Gareth Thomas: 'I have been the victim of a hate crime' |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-wales-46253256 |access-date=18 November 2018 |publisher=[[BBC News]] |date=17 November 2018 }} On 14 September 2019, Thomas announced that he is [[HIV]] positive,{{Cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-wales-49675303 |title=Ex-Wales rugby captain Gareth Thomas reveals he has HIV |last=Davies |first=Daniel |date=14 September 2019 |publisher=[[BBC News]] |access-date=14 September 2019 }}{{Cite web |title=Gareth Thomas forced to reveal HIV diagnosis having kept it secret for years |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-union/news/12321/11810121/gareth-thomas-forced-to-reveal-hiv-diagnosis-having-kept-it-secret-for-years |access-date=2023-09-06 |publisher=Sky Sports |language=en}}{{Cite news |date=2022-03-03 |title=Gareth Thomas: HIV means people still afraid to hug me |language=en-GB |publisher=BBC News |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-wales-60601063 |access-date=2023-09-06}} with [[HIV#Treatment and transmission|undetectable]] status,{{cite news |url=https://www.itv.com/news/2019-09-14/rugby-legend-gareth-thomas-reveals-hiv-positive-status/ |title=Rugby legend Gareth Thomas takes on Ironman challenge to 'break the stigma' around HIV |work=ITV News |date=14 September 2019 |access-date=19 September 2019 }} meaning that he is not infectious.{{cite web |url=https://www.tht.org.uk/news/duke-sussex-and-gareth-thomas-tackle-hiv-stigma-new-film-mark-national-hiv-testing-week |title=Duke of Sussex and Gareth Thomas tackle HIV stigma in a new film to mark National HIV Testing Week |publisher=[[Terrence Higgins Trust]] |date=16 November 2019 |access-date=2 January 2020 |archive-date=25 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191225213150/https://www.tht.org.uk/news/duke-sussex-and-gareth-thomas-tackle-hiv-stigma-new-film-mark-national-hiv-testing-week |url-status=dead }} On the following day he competed in the [[Ironman triathlon|Ironman]] Wales event in [[Tenby]], finishing 413th out of 2,039, having vowed to "break the stigma" around the illness.{{cite news |url=https://www.itv.com/news/2019-09-15/gareth-thomas-enters-ironman-wales-to-break-the-stigma-around-hiv/ |title=Gareth Thomas hugs husband after finishing Ironman Wales to 'break the stigma' over HIV |publisher=[[ITV News]] |date=15 September 2019 |access-date=19 September 2019 }} After feeling media pressure to do so and for [[National HIV Testing Week]], Thomas also filmed a documentary with [[Prince Harry, Duke of Sussex]] and the [[Terrence Higgins Trust]], which aired a few days later on 18 September. Publicising the documentary that morning, he told an interviewer for [[BBC Radio 5 Live]] that a journalist had revealed his HIV status to his parents before he had had the opportunity to do so himself. While Thomas declined to name the journalist or the paper they write for, he did say "everybody will know, especially of late", leading the ''[[Press Gazette]]'' to suggest it could be ''[[The Sun (United Kingdom)|The Sun]]'', given they had been criticised the previous day for reporting about the family of England cricketer [[Ben Stokes]]. A BBC documentary, ''Gareth Thomas: HIV and Me'', aired in September 2019.{{Cite news|last=Godfrey|first=Chris|date=2020-06-08|title=Gareth Thomas on coming out as HIV positive: 'It was my right to tell my family – not somebody else's'|language=en-GB|work=The Guardian|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2020/jun/08/gareth-thomas-on-coming-out-as-hiv-positive-it-was-my-right-to-tell-my-family-not-somebody-elses|access-date=2020-06-08|issn=0261-3077}}{{Cite web |title=BBC One – Gareth Thomas: HIV and Me |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/m0008yrh |access-date=2023-09-06 |publisher=BBC |language=en-GB}} Thomas was appointed Commander of the [[Order of the British Empire]] (CBE) in the [[2020 Birthday Honours]] for services to sport and health.{{London Gazette|issue=63135|supp=y|page=B10|date=10 October 2020}} In 2022, Thomas was accused of "deceptively" transmitting HIV to a previous partner.{{Cite web |date=2022-08-08 |title=TV and rugby star sued by ex over HIV 'deception' |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-wales-62469709 |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=BBC News |language=en-GB}} Thomas settled the case for £75,000 plus costs "without admitting liability or guilt".{{cite news|publisher= BBC Wales|title=Gareth Thomas: No HIV legal action regrets – rugby star's ex|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-wales-64490155|date=1 February 2023|access-date=11 February 2023}} ==Film== In November 2011, actor [[Mickey Rourke]] said that he was planning to portray Thomas in a movie of his life; the film was due to start filming in February 2013.{{cite news |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/movie-star-mickey-rourke-set-94326 |work=[[Daily Mirror]] |title=Movie star Mickey Rourke set to play Gareth Thomas on the big screen |date=29 November 2011 }} Thomas was later reported to be working with a writer on the script of the biopic.{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-wales-north-east-wales-12149569 |work=[[Daily Mirror]] |title=Rugby star Gareth Thomas talks over Rourke film script| date=10 January 2012}} In March 2012, on the [[RTÉ]] programme ''[[Craig Doyle Live]]'', Thomas announced that [[Tom Hardy]] was in talks to play him in the film. Rourke announced that he had given back the part to the producers. The film project later fell through, but Thomas said in 2015 that he was working on a new film with a different writer.{{cite news |last1=Rees |first1=Jasper |title=Gareth Thomas: how my secret life took centre stage |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/theatre/theatre-features/11421499/Gareth-Thomas-how-my-secret-life-took-centre-stage.html |access-date=21 February 2015 |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London |date=19 February 2015 }} ==Media career== Thomas was an analyst for [[ITV Sport]]'s coverage of the [[Rugby World Cup]] tournaments in [[2011 Rugby World Cup|2011]], [[2015 Rugby World Cup|2015]] and [[2019 Rugby World Cup|2019]]. In January 2012, Thomas was a housemate in the [[Celebrity Big Brother (British series 9)|ninth]] series of ''[[Celebrity Big Brother (British TV series)|Celebrity Big Brother]]'', and finished third overall on the final night.{{cite web |url=http://www.bbc.co.uk/newsbeat/16444336 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120110070749/http://www.bbc.co.uk/newsbeat/16444336 |url-status=dead |archive-date=10 January 2012 |title=Celebrities go into ''Big Brother'' house |date=6 January 2012 |work=[[Newsbeat]] |access-date=9 January 2012 }} The same year, Thomas made a [[cameo appearance]] as himself in an episode of the [[Sky 1]] comedy drama ''[[Stella (UK TV series)|Stella]]''. Thomas is also one of 8 celebrities chosen to participate in an intense week learning Welsh in an eco-friendly chic campsite in Pembrokeshire in the series ''[[cariad@iaith:love4language]]'' shown on [[S4C]] in May 2012. In November 2012, he hinted to an audience of 300 people that he may be appearing in an ice dancing show{{cite web |url=http://www.liveeventsgroup.co.uk/news/gareth-thomas-dancing-on-ice |title=Gareth Thomas – ''Dancing on Ice'' |publisher=Live Events Group |date=24 November 2012 |access-date=17 June 2013 |archive-date=27 November 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241127094017/https://www.liveeventsgroup.co.uk/news/gareth-thomas-dancing-on-ice |url-status=dead }} and in December 2012, he was formally announced as one of the contestants, due to take part in the [[Dancing on Ice (series 8)|eighth series]] of ITV's ''[[Dancing on Ice]]'' in January 2013. His partner was Robin Johnstone. He was at or near the top of the leader board every week. In week 9, he suffered nausea and motion sickness whilst practising his "flying" routine, and was advised not to perform it and afterwards was advised to drop out of the series because of this.{{Citation needed|date=July 2023}} On 18 January 2015, Thomas took part in celebrity talent show ''[[Get Your Act Together]]''.{{Cite web |url=http://www.itv.com/presscentre/ep1week4/get-your-act-together |title=Get Your Act Together Episode 1 | presscentre |access-date=21 January 2015 |archive-date=15 January 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150115093253/http://www.itv.com/presscentre/ep1week4/get-your-act-together |url-status=dead }} He features in the Pinc List of leading Welsh LGBT figures.{{cite web |title=Pinc List 2017 |url=http://www.walesonline.co.uk/whats-on/whats-on-news/pinc-list-2017-40-most-13462359 |website=Wales Online |date=19 August 2017}} In 2017, Thomas participated in [[The Jump (series 4)|the fourth series]] of ''[[The Jump (2014 TV series)|The Jump]]''. He withdrew on 7 March 2017, and [[Lydia Bright]] replaced him for the final. In 2023, Thomas participated in the fifth celebrity series of [[SAS: Who Dares Wins]]. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Gareth Thomas (rugby player)}} *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20090815044639/http://www.wru.co.uk/eng/matchcentre/squads_wales_player_archive.php?player=3700&includeref=dynamic Wales Rugby Union profile] *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20080417030415/http://www.cardiffblues.com/476_521.php Cardiff Blues profile] *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20091227072055/http://officialgareththomas.com/ Official webpage] {{s-start}} {{s-sports}} {{s-bef| before=''[[Brian O'Driscoll]]''
